name: conductor-manage
description: Manage track lifecycle: archive, restore, delete, rename, and cleanup
capabilities: Archive completed tracks to free up active workspace
Restore archived tracks back to active state
Permanently delete tracks with confirmation safety
Rename tracks while maintaining metadata consistency
Detect and clean up orphaned track artifacts
List and filter tracks by status (active, completed, archived)
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 208 static findings are false positives. The skill consists of documentation files (SKILL.md and implementation-playbook.md) - no executable code. The detected patterns are markdown formatting (backticks for file paths), documentation text (words containing 'des' misinterpreted as weak crypto), and example error messages misinterpreted as reconnaissance.
valueStatement: This skill automates track lifecycle management for Conductor workflows, handling archiving, restoration, deletion, renaming, and cleanup of orphaned artifacts across active, completed, and archived states.
limitations: Requires Conductor to be initialized in the repository
Cannot modify tracks without proper file permissions
Does not execute track tasks - only manages lifecycle

useCases.targetUser: Project managers and developers managing multiple workflow tracks
useCases.title: Archive completed workflow tracks
useCases.description: Move finished tracks to archive storage to keep active workspace organized

useCases.targetUser: Team leads reviewing past project completions
useCases.title: Restore archived tracks for review
useCases.description: Bring back previously archived tracks to active state for reference or continued work

useCases.title: Clean up orphaned artifacts
useCases.description: Detect and remove track files that no longer have corresponding entries in tracks.md

promptTemplates.title: List all tracks
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to list all tracks in my Conductor workflow
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick overview of all tracks and their statuses

promptTemplates.title: Archive a completed track
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to archive the completed track with ID 'feature-login_20250115'
promptTemplates.scenario: Archiving a track after completing all tasks

promptTemplates.title: Restore an archived track
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to restore the archived track 'bug-fix_20241201'
promptTemplates.scenario: Bringing back an archived track for further work

promptTemplates.title: Cleanup orphaned artifacts
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to detect and cleanup any orphaned track artifacts
promptTemplates.scenario: Regular maintenance to keep track directory clean

bestPractices: Always backup track data before performing delete operations
Confirm destructive actions like delete and cleanup before execution
Use the --force flag only when you are certain of the operation
Keep archived tracks for audit trail and historical reference
antiPatterns: Using delete without confirming which track will be removed
Skipping the archive step before permanent deletion
Running cleanup without understanding what orphaned means
Renaming tracks without updating all references to the old ID

faq.question: What is Conductor?
faq.answer: Conductor is a workflow management system that helps track projects, tasks, and goals using markdown files and directories.

faq.question: Can I recover a deleted track?
faq.answer: No, permanent deletion cannot be undone. Always use archive instead of delete unless you are certain.

faq.question: What are orphaned artifacts?
faq.answer: Orphaned artifacts are track directories that exist but no longer have a corresponding entry in tracks.md.

faq.question: How do I rename a track safely?
faq.answer: Use the rename function which automatically updates all references in tracks.md and metadata files.

faq.question: Does this skill execute track tasks?
faq.answer: No, this skill only manages the track lifecycle (archive, restore, delete, rename, cleanup). Use other skills to execute actual track work.

faq.question: What happens to git history when I delete a track?
faq.answer: Git commit history is preserved. Only the track directory and its contents are removed.
